info.name: Clan of the Bruiser
info.description: The Bruiser subclass for the Crusader.
info.author: Guzufu

description.p: Protect the wilds with powerful strikes and sickening defences

Some Crusaders forgo the typical arnaments and instead choose to use their body as the weapon against evil beings in the realm. These Crusaders typically still wear the heavy armour associated with their brethren, with more exaggerated gauntlets for additional damage and intimidation.

You are just as adept with fighting with your fists as with any weapon. When you make an Unarmed Strike, you can use a d6 for the damage instead of the usual damage. Also, you can choose to use one of your Weapon Mastery's on giving your unarmed strike either the Push, Topple, Cleave, or Slow properties.

sheet.description: When you make an Unarmed Strike, you can use a d6 for the damage instead of the usual damage. Also, you can choose to use one of your Weapon Mastery's on giving your unarmed strike either the Push, Topple, Cleave, or Slow properties.

description.p: You are able to effectively grapple targets while still keeping up the offence. Attacks you make against a grappled target have advantage.

sheet.description: Attacks you make against a grappled target have advantage.

description.p: Your ability to grapple grows even stronger. When you move with a target that you are grappling, your movement speed is not halved. In addition, a target that is grappled by you takes 1d4 of bludgeoning damage at the start of their turn.

sheet.description: When you move with a target that you are grappling, your movement speed is not halved. A target that is grappled by you takes 1d4 of bludgeoning damage at the start of their turn.

description.p: You are able to daze a target with your hefty strikes. When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you can attempt to daze the target. The target must make a Constitution saving throw against your save DC (8 + your proficiency bonus + your constitution modifier). On a failed save, their speed is halved and all attacks that they make before the start of your next turn have disadvantage. You can use this feature a number of times per long rest equal to your proficiency bonus.

sheet.description: When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you can attempt to daze the target. The target must make a DC {{pushbeyond:dc}} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, their speed is halved and all attacks that they make before the start of your next turn have disadvantage.

description.p: "Your skills as a grappler grow even stronger. While you are grappling a target, you can use your action to crush them in your grip, dealing 1d10 + your strength modifier of bludgeoning damage and giving the target disadvantage on their next saving throw against escaping your grapple.

sheet.description: While you are grappling a target, you can use your action to crush them in your grip, dealing 1d10+{{strength:modifier}} of bludgeoning damage and giving the target disadvantage on their next saving throw against escaping your grapple.

element.name: You're Coming With Me Feature Replacement
